Scenario.title: Ransomware Attack on Transportation Infrastructure
Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Complex A sophisticated ransomware campaign targets transportation infrastructure, leveraging unpatched systems and AI-driven reconnaissance to deploy AvosLocker ransomware, encrypting critical operations while evading detection.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Target Artifact (Tasks), Target System Artifact, Internet-facing assets, Windows Registry, OS Process, User Account, Credential Store, File, Target Artifact (Algorithm), Cloud Instance
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Artificial Intelligence, Use of Pre-Packaged Ransomware, Delivery Method, Bash Script, Windows Registry Key Creation, Vulnerabilities, Process Injection, User Account Authentication, Reg.exe Utility, Pass the Hash, File Modification, Encrypted Channel, Process Creation

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker uses AI to identify exposed servers and outdated software in transportation systems, exploiting vulnerabilities in legacy infrastructure.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The threat actor prepares AvosLocker ransomware payloads for deployment, leveraging pre-packaged malware to expedite attacks.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2017-5638 (Suggested): Apache Struts Jakarta Multipart parser allows for malicious file upload using the Content-Type value, leading to remote code execution.
stage.description: The attacker compromises unpatched servers with default credentials via a remote code execution flaw in Apache Struts, establishing initial foothold.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2019-5825 (Suggested): Google Chromium V8 Engine contains an out-of-bounds write vulnerability that allows a remote attacker to potentially exploit heap corruption via a crafted HTML page.
stage.description: The attacker runs AvosLocker ransomware via CLI to execute malicious scripts, avoiding detection by minimizing process visibility.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: A scheduled task is created in the Windows Registry to maintain persistence, ensuring post-reboot access to compromised systems.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attacker exploits unpatched OS components to escalate privileges, gaining administrative access to critical systems.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: Malicious code is injected into legitimate processes to hide ransomware activities from monitoring tools.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: Password spraying is used to extract credentials from memory, enabling lateral movement across the network.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attacker maps internal network topology by sniffing traffic, identifying key systems and pathways for further exploitation.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC-461: Web Services API Signature Forgery Leveraging Hash Function Extension Weakness
stage.description: Stolen credentials are used to move laterally across systems, expanding the attack surface within the transportation network.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: Operational logs and control system files are targeted for destruction, disrupting transportation operations.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: Data is exfiltrated covertly over HTTPS, masking the transfer within legitimate traffic patterns.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-670: Software Development Tools Maliciously Altered
stage.description: Critical transportation infrastructure systems are encrypted using AvosLocker, crippling operations and demanding ransom payments.

Scenario.summary: A complex ransomware campaign leverages AI-driven reconnaissance, unpatched vulnerabilities, and pre-packaged malware to infiltrate transportation systems. By combining privilege escalation, lateral movement, and encrypted exfiltration, the attacker disrupts operations and demands payment, highlighting the risks of outdated infrastructure and weak security practices.
